Review and update the Data Analyst README to reflect: (1) all enrichment candidates are accessed via <code>properties WHERE archived=1</code>, never via deletion; (2) analyst sets <code>archived=1</code> with a descriptive <code>archive_reason</code> for any record they cannot immediately verify; (3) analyst never deletes from <code>archived_properties</code> or <code>properties</code> without user approval per DATA_GUARDRAILS Rule 2.</td><td>Done</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>

(1) Add a GitHub Actions cron job (cron: &#x27;0 9 * * 1&#x27; — every Monday 09:00 UTC) to run <code>make sync</code> and commit the updated data. (2) Add a cron job to refresh <code>macro_trend.json</code> with latest BoE rate, HPI, and market volume data on the same weekly schedule. (3) Add a freshness indicator to the dashboard: in MarketPulse, show days-since-last-refresh with a red/amber/green indicator if data is &gt;7/&gt;3/&gt;0 days old. <tr><td><strong>DE-164</strong></td><td>Data Engineer: Auto-merge new links and visuals on duplicate properties in sync pipeline. Currently, sync_data.js uses upsert logic (matched on address+area) that correctly preserves analyst fields (alpha_score, analyst_notes) but SILENTLY DROPS new data: (1) New links from a re-seen property are discarded — the UPDATE statement never touches the links column; (2) New gallery images, floorplan_url, and streetview_url from fresh scrapes are also dropped; (3) Higher-resolution image_url is not promoted. Fix: (a) On duplicate detection, merge new links into the existing links array (deduplicate by URL) before UPDATE; (b) Add missing gallery images to the existing gallery array; (c) Update image_url if new value is ≥1024px and existing is lower resolution or missing; (d) Add missing floorplan_url and streetview_url. The UPDATE statement in sync_data.js (line ~211) must be expanded to include these merge-logic fields. <tr><td><strong>DAT-172</strong></td><td>Research: Manual sqft and lease verification — Cloudflare block prevents automated scraping. Properties requiring manual portal research: (1) Kenmore Court NW6 725k SoF, (2) St Georges Ave N7 650k SoF — both need sqft ≥600. (3) Southgate Road N1 550k Leasehold, (4) Alwyne Road N1 695k Leasehold — both need remaining lease term ≥90yr. Zoopla Cloudflare protection blocks all automated sqft/lease extraction. Agent websites also blocked. Manual research required via portal login or direct agent inquiry.</td><td>Done</td><td>2026-04-02</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>DAT-173</strong></td><td>Data Analyst: Classify all 31 archived properties by structured market_status. Currently archived records have free-text archive_reason strings — impossible to filter or act on in the UI. Scope: (1) Add a structured market_status column to the properties table: ALTER TABLE properties ADD COLUMN market_status TEXT DEFAULT &#x27;unknown&#x27; CHECK(market_status IN (&#x27;active&#x27;,&#x27;under_offer&#x27;,&#x27;sold_stc&#x27;,&#x27;sold_completed&#x27;,&#x27;withdrawn&#x27;,&#x27;unknown&#x27;)); (2) For each of the 31 archived records, review the archive_reason and assign the correct market_status: &#x27;Off-Market — Let Agreed/Listing Removed/Wrong Channel/New Build Sold/Stale/Student Let&#x27; → &#x27;withdrawn&#x27;; &#x27;Off-Market — Reclassified Overseas&#x27; → &#x27;withdrawn&#x27;; &#x27;Cannot Verify — Discard&#x27; (off-market reasons) → &#x27;withdrawn&#x27;; &#x27;Pre-enrichment Duplicate&#x27; → &#x27;unknown&#x27; (duplicate, not a market signal); &#x27;Still Active — Rightmove RES_BUY&#x27; (5 records) → &#x27;active&#x27;; &#x27;Data Discrepancy&#x27; → &#x27;unknown&#x27;; (3) Populate market_status for ALL active (non-archived) records as &#x27;active&#x27; — properties in the pipeline that are still listed are by definition active; (4) Document the taxonomy in agents/data_analyst/README.md under a new &#x27;Market Status Classification&#x27; section; (5) Run a verification query to confirm all 73 records have a market_status value.</td><td>Done</td><td>2026-04-02</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>DE-165</strong></td><td>Data Engineer: Add market_status column and last_checked to SQLite schema and sync pipeline. (1) Schema: ALTER TABLE properties ADD COLUMN market_status TEXT DEFAULT &#x27;unknown&#x27;; ALTER TABLE properties ADD COLUMN last_checked TEXT; (note: if ALTER TABLE fails due to SQLite version, use the migration-while-copy approach used for previous schema changes); (2) Sync pipeline (scripts/sync_data.js): on INSERT of a new property, set market_status=&#x27;active&#x27; and last_checked to today&#x27;s date. On UPDATE of an existing property (upsert match), update last_checked to today&#x27;s date. On re-seeing a property from a scraper, set last_checked. (3) API: GET /api/properties should return market_status and last_checked in the response. GET /api/properties/:id should return both. (4) Add a /api/properties/:id/check endpoint: POST to re-check a property — updates last_checked to today, re-fetches from source if available, returns updated record.</td><td>Done</td><td>2026-04-02</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>FE-185</strong></td><td>UI: Build Archive Review page and surface market_status + last_checked throughout the UI. Part 1 — Archive Review page (/properties?status=archived or dedicated /archive route): (1) Filter archived properties by market_status — show status groups: &#x27;Active — Recheck Needed&#x27; (amber), &#x27;Withdrawn&#x27; (muted), &#x27;Sold / STC&#x27; (muted), &#x27;Unknown&#x27; (neutral). Make &#x27;Active — Recheck Needed&#x27; prominent — these are properties the analyst flagged as still listed but with incomplete data; (2) Each archived property card shows: market_status badge, last_checked date (or &#x27;Never verified&#x27;), archive_reason, Alpha Score, address, area, last seen price; (3) &#x27;Recheck&#x27; action button on each card — calls POST /api/properties/:id/check to update last_checked and re-fetch listing status; (4) &#x27;Reactivate&#x27; action — sets archived=0, market_status=&#x27;active&#x27;, navigates to property detail. (5) Filter bar: market_status dropdown, last_checked range (e.g. &#x27;Not checked in 30+ days&#x27;), archive_reason text search. Part 2 — Active property cards (non-archived): show market_status=&#x27;active&#x27; badge (green, small), show last_checked freshness indicator. Part 3 — PropertyDetail: show market_status badge and last_checked date. Part 4 — PropertyTable and PropertyRow: for archived rows, show market_status badge alongside the archive icon.</td><td>Done</td><td>2026-04-02</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>QA-184</strong></td><td>QA: Audit all 31 archived properties for archive_reason accuracy. After DAT-172 (market_status classification), verify: (1) All &#x27;Still Active&#x27; records are correctly marked market_status=&#x27;active&#x27;; (2) No &#x27;active&#x27; market_status records are genuinely withdrawn or sold; (3) All records have a last_checked date; (4) Produce a written report (agents/ui_ux_qa/ARCHIVE_AUDIT_2026_04_02.md) listing: each property, its current archive_reason, its assigned market_status, the rationale, and any discrepancies found; (5) Flag any records where the archive_reason text is ambiguous or missing sufficient rationale.</td><td>Done</td><td>2026-04-02</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>FE-186</strong></td><td>UI + Data Engineer: Sync pipeline status to backend so it persists across sessions and survives browser data clearing. Currently <code>usePipeline.ts</code> stores pipeline status (discovered/shortlisted/vetted/archived) in localStorage only — it is not written to SQLite and is lost if the browser is cleared. (1) Data Engineer: Add <code>pipeline_status TEXT</code> column to the <code>properties</code> table (ALTER TABLE or migration-while-copy). Add <code>PATCH /api/properties/:id/status</code> endpoint accepting <code>{ pipeline_status: string }</code> — validates against <code>discovered|shortlisted|vetted|archived</code>, writes to the new column, returns the updated record. (2) Frontend: Update <code>usePipeline.ts</code> so <code>setStatus()</code> calls <code>PATCH /api/properties/:id/status</code> AND updates localStorage as a fallback. On app load, hydrate localStorage from the backend API (GET /api/properties returns pipeline_status per record). This ensures pipeline decisions persist server-side and survive browser resets. (3) Update the analyst README (agents/data_analyst/README.md) to remove the &#x27;archived records&#x27; section — analyst no longer sets archived; that column is now exclusively for the user&#x27;s pipeline decisions. The analyst uses <code>analyst_flag</code> and <code>analyst_notes</code> instead. (4) Update REQUIREMENTS.md section 4 to reflect that pipeline state is now backend-persisted.</td><td>Done</td><td>2026-04-02</td></tr>
